items.description:
A single entry in the `[[auth]]` array of `.weaver.toml`.

```toml
[[auth]]
url_prefix = "https://github.com/acme/"
token_env  = "ACME_GITHUB_TOKEN"

[[auth]]
url_prefix    = "https://github.com/"
token_command = ["gh", "auth", "token"]
```

Exactly one of `token`, `token_env`, or `token_command` must be specified.

name.description: Optional diagnostic name; not used for matching.

url_prefix.description: Match any URL whose string starts with this prefix. Longest prefix wins.

oneOf.description:
Literal token. Discouraged — prefer `TokenEnv` or `TokenCommand` so the
token does not end up committed in `.weaver.toml`.
Name of an environment variable to read at fetch time.

Argv of a helper command; its first stdout line is the token. The
standard answer for short-lived tokens (e.g. `["gh", "auth", "token"]`).

check.description: Validate a semantic convention registry against policies and schema rules.

stdout.description: Send diagnostic output to stdout instead of stderr.

template.description: Path to the directory where the diagnostic templates are located.

diff.description: Compare two versions of a semantic convention registry and report the differences.

format.description:
Format used to render the schema changes. Predefined formats are: ansi, json,
and markdown.
[default: ansi]

output.description:
Path to the directory where the generated artifacts will be saved.
If not specified, the diff report is printed to stdout

templates.description:
Path to the directory where the schema changes templates are located.
[default: diff_templates]

emit.description: Emit a resolved registry as OTLP or to stdout.

endpoint.description:
Endpoint for the OTLP receiver. OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var will override this.
[default: http://localhost:4317]

stdout.description:
Write the telemetry to standard output
[default: false]

generate.description: Generate artifacts from a semantic convention registry using Jinja templates.

output.description:
Path to the directory where the generated artifacts will be saved.
Default is the `output` directory.

target.description: Target to generate the artifacts for.

templates.description:
Path to the directory where the templates are located.
Default is the `templates` directory.
[default: templates]

infer.description: Infer a semantic convention registry by observing live OTLP telemetry.

admin_port.description:
Port used by the HTTP admin server (endpoints: /stop).
[default: 8080]

grpc_address.description:
Address used by the gRPC OTLP listener.
[default: 0.0.0.0]

grpc_port.description:
Port used by the gRPC OTLP listener.
[default: 4317]

inactivity_timeout.description:
Seconds of inactivity before auto-stop (0 = never).
[default: 60]

output.description:
Output folder for generated YAML files.
[default: ./inferred-registry/]

live-check.description: Validate live telemetry against a semantic convention registry.

advice_data.description:
Glob pattern pointing to additional JSON/YAML files to load into OPA rego data.
Files are nested in OPA data using their relative path inside the glob base directory (e.g. schemas/user.json is loaded at data.user).

advice_policies.description: Advice policies directory. Overrides the built-in default policies.

advice_preprocessor.description:
Advice preprocessor — a jq script run once over the registry data before
being passed to rego policies.

fail_on.description:
Severity threshold that causes a non-zero exit code. Findings at this
level or higher fail the run. Use `none` to never fail.

finding_filters.description:
Filters control which findings are dropped. A filter without `signal_type`
applies globally; a filter with `signal_type` applies only to that signal type.

format.description:
Format used to render the report.
Builtin formats: `json`, `yaml`, `jsonl`. Other values are template names
(e.g. `ansi` selects the ansi templates).

input_format.description: The format of the input telemetry. `text` | `json`. (Not used for OTLP.)

input_source.description: Where to read the input telemetry from. `{file path}` | `stdin` | `otlp`.

no_stats.description: Disable statistics accumulation. Useful for long-running live-check sessions.

no_stream.description: Disable stream mode. When set, the report is built up before being rendered.

output.description:
Path to the directory where the generated artifacts will be saved.
`none` disables all template output rendering.
`http` sends the report as the response to the `/stop` request on the admin port.

templates.description: Path to the directory where the templates are located.

mcp.description: Expose a semantic convention registry over the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

advice_data.description:
Glob pattern pointing to additional JSON/YAML files to load into OPA rego data.
Files are nested in OPA data using their relative path inside the glob base directory (e.g. schemas/user.json is loaded at data.user).

advice_policies.description: Advice policies directory. Set this to override the default policies.

advice_preprocessor.description: Advice preprocessor. A jq script to preprocess the registry data before passing to rego.

namespace_separator.description:
Namespace separator used in attribute keys. Defaults to ".".
Used by namespace browsing and search token splitting.
[default: .]

package.description: Package a resolved registry for publication (produces `resolved.yaml` and `manifest.yaml`).

output.description:
Path to the directory where the package will be written.
[default: output]

resolved_registry_uri.description:
URI where the resolved registry artifact will eventually be published.
This value is embedded in the publication manifest as `resolved_registry_uri`.

policy.description: Policy configuration — which policy files to check against.

paths.description: Policy file or directory paths. Directories load all `.rego` files.

registry.description: Registry configuration — where to load the semantic convention registry from.

follow_symlinks.description: Follow symlinks when loading the registry.

include_unreferenced.description:
Include signals and attributes from dependency registries even if
not explicitly referenced.

path.description: Local folder, Git repo URL, or Git archive URL.

serve.description: Serve a resolved registry over a local HTTP API for browsing and search.

bind.description:
Address to bind the server to.
[default: 127.0.0.1:8080]

cors_origins.description:
Allowed CORS origins (comma-separated). Use '*' for any origin.
If not specified, CORS is disabled (same-origin only).

stats.description: Compute and display statistics about a semantic convention registry.

format.description:
Output format for the stats.
Predefined formats are: text, json, yaml, jsonl, mute.
[default: text]

output.description:
Path to the directory where the generated artifacts will be saved.
If not specified, the stats are printed to stdout.

templates.description:
Path to the directory where the stats templates are located.
[default: stats_templates]

acronyms.description: List of acronyms treated as unmodifiable words during case conversion.

text_maps.description:
Named text mappings used by the `map_text` filter (e.g. a
`namespace_mapping` from `CICD` to `CI/CD`).

update-markdown.description: Update Jinja-marker sections inside Markdown files from a resolved registry.

attribute_registry_base_url.description:
Optional path to the attribute registry.
If provided, all attributes will be linked here.

dry_run.description:
Whether or not to run updates in dry-run mode.
A bare `--dry-run` means `true`; when passing an explicit value, the
`=` form is required (e.g. `--dry-run=false`).
[default: false]

markdown_dir.description: Path to the directory where the markdown files are located.

target.description:
The target to generate snippets with.
Note: `registry update-markdown` will look for a specific jinja template:
  {templates}/{target}/snippet.md.j2, or {templates}/registry/{target}/snippet.md.j2
  if a `registry` subdirectory is present.

templates.description:
Path to the directory where the templates are located.
Note: `registry update-markdown` will look for a specific jinja template:
  {templates}/{target}/snippet.md.j2, or {templates}/registry/{target}/snippet.md.j2
  if a `registry` subdirectory is present.
[default: templates]

FailOnLevel.description:
Severity gate controlling when `registry live-check` exits non-zero.

Supported thresholds (highest → lowest severity): `Violation`,
`Improvement`, `Information`, `None`. A finding whose level is at or above
the chosen threshold causes a non-zero exit code. `None` disables the gate.

oneOf.description: Fail on any finding (information, improvement, or violation).

Never fail based on findings — always exit 0 unless an internal error
occurs.

FindingFilter.description:
A filter that drops findings by ID exclusion or minimum level.
Optional `signal_type` scopes the filter to a specific signal type.

exclude_samples.description:
Drop all findings for samples with these names.
For attribute samples, this matches the attribute key — e.g.
`["trace.parent_id", "trace.span_id"]` suppresses all findings
(e.g. `missing_attribute`) for those attribute keys.

min_level.description: Drop all findings below this level.

signal_type.description:
Optional signal type scope. When set, this filter only applies to
findings with a matching signal_type.

oneOf.description: Suggested change that would improve things.

LiveCheckEmitConfig.description: OTLP log emission settings for live-check.

otlp_logs.description: Enable OTLP log emission for live-check policy findings.

otlp_logs_stdout.description: Use stdout for OTLP log emission (debug mode).

admin_port.description: Port used by the HTTP admin port (endpoints: `/stop`, `/health`).

grpc_address.description: Address used by the gRPC OTLP listener.

grpc_port.description: Port used by the gRPC OTLP listener.

inactivity_timeout.description: Max inactivity time in seconds before stopping the listener.
